extraction.one_line_summary: PINOT Thibaut attacked 6km from summit of Lagos de Covadonga while GC favorites marked each other, winning by 28 seconds.
tactical_patterns: PINOT exploited GC stalemate by attacking from non-threatening overall position, gaining 28s while favorites watched each other.
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel counterattacked after PINOT established gap, but YATES refused to chase without QUINTANA/VALVERDE cooperation.
GARCÍA CORTINA fed breakaway teammate EZQUERRA Jesús late in stage, suggesting Bahrain sacrificed breakaway GC placement for team classification.
Astana paced category one climb mid-stage despite no visible GC interest, possibly burning matches for LÓPEZ's late attack.

rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: attacked from non-threatening GC position 6km out, banking on favorites' mutual distrust to avoid chase
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: only GC contender willing to counterattack PINOT alone, showing superior form to passive rivals
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: frustrated with QUINTANA/VALVERDE refusal to share chase work, suggesting defensive rather than dominant position
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: dropped on final climb despite moderate pace, indicating yesterday's efforts took toll
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: negative: distanced early on final climb, struggling to maintain high mountain form over consecutive days
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: neutral: followed favorites to finish but showed no attacking ambition despite being within striking distance
rider_signals.MAS Enric: neutral: finished with lead group but passive throughout, content to limit losses
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: briefly dropped then returned to lead group, suggesting variable pacing rather than weakness
